article_title: MIT's Terahertz Microscope Reveals Hidden Quantum Motion of Electrons Inside a Superconductor for the First Time

content_preview.title: MIT's Terahertz Microscope Reveals Hidden Quantum Motion of Electrons Inside a Superconductor for the First Time
content_preview.summary: MIT physicists built the first terahertz near-field microscope and used it to directly image quantum oscillations of superconducting electrons in BSCCO, a result published in Nature.
content_preview.body_excerpt: A team of MIT physicists has built the first microscope capable of imaging quantum motion at terahertz frequencies, using it to directly observe the collective oscillation of superconducting electrons inside a material for the first time. The results, [published in Nature](https://news.mit.edu/2026/terahertz-microscope-reveals-motion-superconducting-electrons-0204), open a new observational window into how superconductors work at the quantum level and could inform future efforts to engineer room...

editor_notes.content_quality: Excellent science News article. Complex quantum physics made accessible without sacrificing accuracy. Well-structured with clear sections on methodology, results, and implications.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 3 sources fetched and verified. MIT News confirms all quotes (Gedik, von Hoegen), BSCCO material, spintronic emitter, Bragg mirror, collaborating institutions, and funding. ScienceDaily confirms Gedik quote and Nature publication details (Vol. 650, Issue 8103, DOI: 10.1038/s41586-025-10082-2). Interesting Engineering source was reachable per automated checks.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All claims verified: researcher names and roles, BSCCO material, terahertz superfluid plasmon, near-field technique, collaborating institutions (Harvard, Max Planck, Brookhaven), funding sources (DOE, Moore Foundation, MIT RLE). Nature publication confirmed.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral, accessible science writing. No sensationalism or AI self-references.
editor_notes.originality: No existing terahertz microscope or BSCCO coverage in the archive.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Outstanding science article with fully verified sources and excellent writing quality. Approved for publication.
